text: This paper explores the implementation of bilingual Czech-Polish nomenclature across the railway network of the Zaolzie region. By assessing the current socio-cultural standing of the Polish national minority, the study examines the role of key advocacy groups—most notably the Congress of Poles in the Czech Republic—in promoting linguistic diversity. While the research outlines the theoretical and legal frameworks governing bilingualism in the Czech Republic, its primary focus is a practical analysis of how bilingual signage has been introduced at railway stations. Beyond physical signage, the study investigates the broader use of the Polish language in public transit, including its integration into timetables and station announcements. Finally, the paper highlights the persistent challenges and systemic obstacles that continue to hinder the full realization of bilingualism in the region’s transport infrastructure.
